description: Managed Identity Holds a Strong Role on a Subscription
rationale: A Managed Identity is a service principal whose credentials are managed by Azure and handed to a compute resource (VM, Function App, Logic App, Container App, etc.). When a Managed Identity holds a strong Azure RBAC role (Owner, Contributor, User Access Administrator, or a custom role with broad/wildcard actions) at subscription scope, anyone who can control the compute resource's control plane - or run code on it - can obtain a token for that Managed Identity (e.g. via the instance metadata endpoint) and act with its subscription-level power. This turns control over a single VM/function into control over the whole subscription, a common cloud lateral-movement and privilege-escalation path.
remediation: Apply least privilege to Managed Identities: replace broad built-in roles (Owner/Contributor/User Access Administrator) with narrower built-in or custom roles scoped to only the resources and actions the workload needs, ideally at resource-group or resource scope rather than subscription scope. Restrict who can deploy code to, or manage, the compute resources that carry powerful Managed Identities, and monitor their token usage.
dashboard_name: Enterprise Applications With Strong Subscription Roles
